name: agileagentcanvas
description: Visual AI-assisted product development with BMAD methodology - Design epics, stories, and requirements collaboratively with interactive AI chat workflows

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_read_file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads a file from the BMAD framework folder or the project output folder. Use this to read agent definitions, workflow steps, schemas, checklists, and any other BMAD framework files.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to read

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_list_directory
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists the contents of a directory inside the BMAD framework folder. Use this to discover available workflows, agents, schemas, and steps.

path.description: Absolute path to the directory to list

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_update_artifact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Saves changes to a BMAD artifact (vision, epic, story, requirement, etc.) in the project. Call this when you have completed refining an artifact and are ready to persist the changes.

type.description: Artifact type: vision, epic, story, requirement, requirements

id.description: Artifact ID, e.g. EPIC-1, STORY-1-1, vision-1, FR-1

changes.description: The fields to update on the artifact, as a JSON object conforming to the artifact schema

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_graph_query
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a natural-language question against the graphify knowledge graph of the codebase. Use this to understand code structure, trace connections between components, or surface design rationale. Requires graphify-out/graph.json to exist.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_graph_path
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Find the shortest path between two nodes in the graphify knowledge graph. Use this to trace dependencies or understand how two components relate. Requires graphify-out/graph.json.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_read_jira
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads epics and stories from the user's Jira Cloud project via the REST API. Use this when the user asks about their Jira board, wants to see Jira issues, or asks about epics/stories in Jira. Requires Jira to be configured in VS Code Settings (agileagentcanvas.jira.*).

epicKey.description: Epic issue key, e.g. PROJ-42 (for list_stories)

chatParticipants.name: agileagentcanvas
chatParticipants.description: AI Business Analyst for product development - helps design epics, stories, and requirements

commands.name: vision
commands.description: Define product vision and problem statement

commands.name: requirements
commands.description: Extract and organize requirements from PRD

commands.name: epics
commands.description: Design epic structure organized by user value

commands.name: stories
commands.description: Break down epics into implementable stories

commands.name: enhance
commands.description: Add verbose details: use cases, fit criteria, risks

commands.name: review
commands.description: Review and validate artifact completeness

commands.name: refine
commands.description: Refine a specific artifact with AI suggestions

commands.name: dev
commands.description: Start development workflow for a story, epic, or test case

commands.name: convert-to-json
commands.description: Convert markdown artifacts to structured JSON format

commands.name: workflows
commands.description: Browse all 44 BMAD workflows by module or artifact type

commands.name: continue
commands.description: Continue to the next step in the current workflow

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show current workflow session status

commands.name: apply
commands.description: Apply pending AI refinements to the artifact JSON file

commands.name: sprint
commands.description: Sprint planning from epics or check sprint status

commands.name: ux
commands.description: Create UX design specifications through collaborative exploration

commands.name: readiness
commands.description: Check implementation readiness of PRD, architecture, epics and stories

commands.name: party
commands.description: Multi-agent collaboration mode — all BMAD agents discuss your topic together

commands.name: document
commands.description: Document a brownfield project for AI context (full scan or deep dive)

commands.name: review-code
commands.description: Adversarial code review finding specific issues

commands.name: ci
commands.description: Scaffold CI/CD quality pipeline with test execution and quality gates

commands.name: quick
commands.description: Quick spec + dev flow for small changes (spec or dev mode)

commands.name: design-thinking
commands.description: Guide human-centered design using empathy-driven methodologies

commands.name: innovate
commands.description: Identify disruption opportunities and architect business model innovation

commands.name: solve
commands.description: Apply systematic problem-solving methodologies to complex challenges

commands.name: story-craft
commands.description: Craft compelling narratives using storytelling frameworks

commands.name: elicit
commands.description: Apply an advanced elicitation method to an artifact and save results

commands.name: context
commands.description: Generate an LLM-optimized project-context.md with implementation rules and patterns

commands.name: write-doc
commands.description: Write a document with the Tech Writer agent following documentation best practices

commands.name: mermaid
commands.description: Generate a Mermaid diagram with the Tech Writer agent

commands.name: readme
commands.description: Generate or update a README.md from project analysis

commands.name: changelog
commands.description: Generate changelog or release notes from git history and project changes

commands.name: api-docs
commands.description: Generate API documentation from source code (OpenAPI, JSDoc, etc.)

commands.name: jira
commands.description: Read epics and stories from Jira Cloud. Subcommands: config, epics [projectKey], stories [epicKey|projectKey], sync [projectKey]

commands.name: graph
commands.description: Show the graphify knowledge graph report (god nodes, communities, suggested questions)

commands.name: graph-query
commands.description: Query the codebase knowledge graph with a natural-language question

commands.name: graph-bootstrap
commands.description: Install graphify and build the initial knowledge graph for this project

commands.name: graph-update
commands.description: Incrementally update the graphify knowledge graph (code-only, no LLM re-extraction)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Describe what you want to do and the extension will recommend the best skills and agents for your task

viewsWelcome.contents:
Open the visual canvas to get started.
[$(preview) Open Visual Canvas](command:agileagentcanvas.openCanvas)
Or create a new project.
[$(add) New Project](command:agileagentcanvas.newProject)
[$(folder-opened) Load Existing Project](command:agileagentcanvas.loadProject)
[$(folder) Switch / Browse Project Folder](command:agileagentcanvas.switchProject)

agileagentcanvas.userCataloguePath.description: Absolute path to a folder containing user-managed skills and agents. Skills placed here are merged with the built-in catalogue; a skill with the same folder name overrides the built-in. Leave empty to use built-ins only. This is a global (user-level) setting shared across all workspaces.

agileagentcanvas.skillRepos.description: List of git repository URLs that contain skill folders to clone into your user catalogue. Each repo is cloned under <userCataloguePath>/_repos/<slug>/. Use the Skill Catalogue UI to manage repos.

agileagentcanvas.outputFolder.description: Name of the subfolder (inside your workspace) where Agile Agent Canvas stores artifacts. Change this to use a custom folder name instead of the default. You can also use the folder button in the canvas toolbar to browse for any folder or create a new one.

agileagentcanvas.autoSync.description: Automatically sync visual changes to files

agileagentcanvas.showAICursor.description: Show AI cursor position in canvas

agileagentcanvas.defaultAgent.description: Default BMAD agent for chat interactions

enumDescriptions: Automatically detect the best available provider (tries VS Code LM API first, then Antigravity, then direct API key)
GitHub Copilot via VS Code Language Model API
OpenAI API (requires agileagentcanvas.apiKey and optionally agileagentcanvas.modelId)
Anthropic Claude API (requires agileagentcanvas.apiKey and optionally agileagentcanvas.modelId)
Google Gemini API (requires agileagentcanvas.apiKey and optionally agileagentcanvas.modelId)
Ollama local models (requires agileagentcanvas.baseUrl, e.g. http://localhost:11434)
Google Antigravity IDE — injects prompts into the Antigravity chat panel via native command
agileagentcanvas.aiProvider.description: AI provider for Agile Agent Canvas. Use 'auto' to detect automatically.

agileagentcanvas.apiKey.description: API key for the selected AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini). Leave empty when using Copilot, Ollama, or Antigravity.

agileagentcanvas.modelId.description: Model ID to use. Defaults: OpenAI=gpt-4o, Anthropic=claude-sonnet-4-5, Gemini=gemini-2.0-flash, Ollama=llama3. Leave empty to use the default for your provider.

agileagentcanvas.baseUrl.description: Base URL for the AI API. Only needed for Ollama (e.g. http://localhost:11434) or a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

agileagentcanvas.logLevel.description: Controls extension logging verbosity. Use info/warn/error for normal use; debug for troubleshooting.

agileagentcanvas.jira.baseUrl.description: Your Jira Cloud base URL, e.g. https://mycompany.atlassian.net (no trailing slash). Required for Jira integration.

agileagentcanvas.jira.email.description: Your Atlassian account email address. Used for Jira API authentication.

agileagentcanvas.jira.apiToken.markdownDeprecationMessage: **Deprecated:** Storing the API token in plain-text settings is insecure. Use the command **Agile Agent Canvas: Set Jira API Token** (`agileagentcanvas.setJiraToken`) instead — it stores the token in the OS keychain. If a value is present here it will be migrated automatically on next use.
agileagentcanvas.jira.apiToken.description: [Deprecated — use setJiraToken command] Your Atlassian API token. Tokens expire after 1 year.

agileagentcanvas.jira.projectKey.description: Default Jira project key to use when none is specified (e.g. PROJ). You can override this per command.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.pythonPath.description: Path to the Python executable used to run graphify (python -m graphify). Change this if Python is not on your PATH, e.g. C:/Python311/python.exe or python3.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.backend.description: AI backend for graphify semantic extraction. Leave empty to use graphify's default. Passed as --backend flag to 'graphify extract'.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.autoBootstrapO.description: Prompt to bootstrap graphify when a workspace with BMAD artifacts but no knowledge graph is opened.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.autoUpdateOnSa.description: Automatically run 'graphify update' when source files change (debounced 5 s). Code-only — no LLM calls. Requires a graph to already exist.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.showCodebaseLa.description: Show the Codebase lane on the canvas when a graphify knowledge graph is present.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Agile Agent Canvas
walkthroughs.description: Set up AI-assisted agile development with BMAD workflows and a code-aware knowledge graph — in minutes.

steps.title: Open the Agile Canvas
steps.description:
The canvas is your visual workspace for BMAD artifacts — epics, stories, vision, architecture, and more.

[Open Canvas](command:agileagentcanvas.openCanvas)

steps.title: Build a Codebase Knowledge Graph
steps.description:
graphify builds a semantic knowledge graph of your codebase so AI suggestions are automatically code-aware. Bootstrap once — it stays up to date automatically.

[Bootstrap graphify](command:agileagentcanvas.graphify.bootstrap)

steps.title: Start AI-Assisted Development
steps.description:
Use **@agileagentcanvas** in Copilot Chat to design epics, break down stories, review code, plan sprints — all grounded in your actual codebase and BMAD artifacts.

[Open Copilot Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open)

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run bundle && npm run compile-webview
